scenario: A company uses a canvas app to perform business logic and process multiple rows of data from a 3rd party SaaS, which provides a standard Power Platform connector. You decide to utilize a Power Automate cloud flow to retrieve the data. You need to call the cloud flow from the app and return multiple rows of data from the flow to the app.
q: How should you configure the flow? Select the appropriate options from the drop-down menus.

rows.s: The trigger to use when calling a cloud flow from a canvas app
rows.a: When Power Apps calls a flow (V2)
options: When Power Apps calls a flow (V2)

rows.s: The action to use when returning data back from the cloud flow to the canvas app
options: Respond to a Power App or a flow

rows.s: The function to use to capture the data returned from the cloud flow in the canvas app

why: Use the 'When Power Apps calls a flow (V2)' instant trigger to call a cloud flow from a canvas app (it lets data be passed from the app and is initiated with the Run command). Use the 'Respond' action (from the Response connector) to return data - it passes back a JSON body that can contain an ARRAY of data (multiple rows); 'Respond to a Power App or a flow' can only return a single item, not multiple rows. In the canvas app, use the 'Collect' function to capture the returned array of data into a collection. 'Run a child flow'/'Manually trigger a flow' cannot be called from a canvas app, and Compose is a Power Automate data-operation action, not a Power Fx function.
